skill.name: codex-pet
skill.description: Generate a custom Codex Pet spritesheet and manifest from a single reference image using RunComfy and ImageMagick. Produces a 1536x1872 atlas with 9 animation rows that Codex recognizes as a custom desktop pet, without requiring Codex Pro or the imagegen system skill.
skill.summary: Generate custom pixel-art Codex Pets with a single image input, using RunComfy GPT Image 2 and ImageMagick. No Codex Pro required.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: All 149 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill consists of a single SKILL.md documentation file with no executable code. Findings for external commands, network, filesystem, and env access are all documentation content: bash examples inside code blocks, descriptive URLs, installation path references, and environment variable names mentioned in text. No runtime behavior or security threats exist. The skill is safe to publish.

critical_findings.title: Heuristic Dangerous Combination
critical_findings.description: Static scanner flagged a heuristic combination of code execution, network access, and credential access. All components are false positives: the 'code execution' is bash examples inside markdown code blocks, 'network' is documentation URLs (runcomfy.com, GitHub), and 'credentials' are environment variable names mentioned in descriptive text. The skill is a pure documentation file with no executable logic.
critical_findings.confidence_reasoning: The heuristic combination is built entirely from false-positive pattern matches on documentation text. No executable code exists in the skill.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at multiple lines. All are false positives triggered by YAML frontmatter delimiters (---), YAML multiline indicators (>), dimension strings (192x208, 1536x1872), package manager names (brew, apt-get), and non-cryptographic symbols (* in size strings). No cryptographic operations exist in this documentation skill.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every match is a false positive from non-cryptographic text patterns in markdown documentation. No cryptographic code exists.

high_findings.title: Path Traversal and Hidden File Access (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged path traversal at line 114 (SOURCE_URL variable in bash example) and hidden file access at line 342 (~/.config/runcomfy/token.json in Security & Privacy documentation). Both are documentation references, not executable code. The skill does not perform any filesystem operations.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both flagged lines are inside documentation text describing user setup. No filesystem code is executed by the skill.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 97 instances of shell command patterns (backticks, command substitution). All are inside markdown code blocks showing example bash commands that users would run manually (runcomfy, magick, cp, mkdir). The skill file is documentation only; it contains no executable logic and no actual command execution at runtime.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every match is inside markdown code fences showing example commands for users. The SKILL.md file is processed as documentation only.

medium_findings.title: Environment Variable Access (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged generic API keys at lines 16 and 49. These are descriptive mentions of RUNCOMFY_TOKEN and OPENAI_API_KEY in the skill description text, not actual code that reads environment variables. The skill documentation explains that only RUNCOMFY_TOKEN is needed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both are descriptive mentions of environment variable names in prose text, not executable code accessing secrets.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 8 hardcoded URLs. All are legitimate documentation links: runcomfy.com homepage and docs, GitHub repository references, and the official hatch-pet skill page. The skill does not make any network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs are documentation references. No network calls are performed by the skill.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged system reconnaissance at lines mentioning brew install, apt-get install, and ls. These are installation prerequisite instructions and bash examples in documentation, not system probing code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All matches are installation instructions and bash command examples in documentation. No system reconnaissance is performed.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documentation-Only Skill Misclassified by Static Scanner
dangerous_patterns.description: This skill consists entirely of a single SKILL.md documentation file. All 149 static analysis findings are false positives triggered by pattern matching on markdown content, bash code examples, and descriptive text. The skill contains no executable code, no runtime behavior, and no security threats.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The entire file is a markdown documentation skill with no executable logic. Every security finding is a false positive from documentation content.

content.user_title: Generate Custom Codex Pet Spritesheets from One Image
content.value_statement: Turn any image into a custom pixel-art Codex Pet without needing Codex Pro. This skill uses RunComfy GPT Image 2 and ImageMagick to produce a complete spritesheet and manifest that Codex recognizes as a custom desktop pet.
actual_capabilities: Generate a 1536x1872 spritesheet with 72 frames across 9 animation rows from one source image
Create pet.json manifest files compatible with OpenAI Codex desktop pets
Produce canonical chibi poses using a single GPT Image 2 API call via RunComfy
Assemble animation frames programmatically with ImageMagick micro-transforms
Install finished pets into the local Codex pets directory automatically
Support batch generation from multiple source images for team or community use
limitations: Requires RunComfy CLI account and a valid RUNCOMFY_TOKEN
Requires ImageMagick installed locally on the system
Animation is subtle micro-transforms, not distinct frame-by-frame poses
GPT Image 2 may drift from the source image identity when simplifying to chibi style

use_cases.title: Create a Personal Desktop Pet
use_cases.description: Turn a photo or drawing into a pixel-art companion that floats over your desktop while Codex codes.

use_cases.title: Batch Generate Pet Packs for a Team
use_cases.description: Process a folder of source images to create multiple custom pets for your entire team or community.

use_cases.title: Build Contest-Ready Creative Pets
use_cases.description: Create custom pets for the Codex Pet contest using RunComfy as an alternative model backend instead of Codex Pro imagegen.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a pet from a URL
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Codex Pet from this image: https://example.com/my-character.png. Name it pixel-cat.
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic usage with a single image URL input

prompt_templates.title: Custom style pet with personality
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a Codex Pet from https://example.com/robot.png. Use a cyberpunk style with neon accents. Name it cyber-bot and describe it as a neon-lit helper for late-night coding sessions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate usage with style and description customization

prompt_templates.title: Batch generate from a folder
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a folder of mascot images at ./mascots/. Generate a Codex Pet for each image in the folder and install them all.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced batch processing for multiple pets

prompt_templates.title: Tune animation intensity
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Codex Pet from https://example.com/dog.png named bouncy-pup. Increase the jump height to 12 pixels and make the idle bob more noticeable with 2 pixel shifts.

output_examples.input: Create a Codex Pet from my photo at https://example.com/me.jpg and name it pixel-me.
output_examples.output: Generated pixel-me pet with 9 animation rows with a total of 72 frames across idle, running-right, running-left, waving, jumping, failed, waiting, running, and review states. Installed to ~/.codex/pets/pixel-me/ with pet.json manifest and spritesheet.webp atlas at 1536x1872 pixels.

output_examples.input: What are the prerequisites for running this skill?
output_examples.output: You need three things: the RunComfy CLI installed via npm, a RunComfy account with a valid RUNCOMFY_TOKEN, and ImageMagick installed on your system. You also need a publicly accessible source image URL in JPEG, PNG, or WebP format.

output_examples.input: My pet has magenta edges around the outline after chroma-key. How do I fix that?
output_examples.output: Increase the fuzz percentage in the magick transparent command from 18% to 25% to remove wider anti-aliased edges. If the pet has near-magenta colors, switch the chroma-key from magenta to cyan or green in both the GPT Image 2 prompt and the ImageMagick processing step.

best_practices: Use a high-contrast source image with a clear isolated subject for best identity preservation in the generated pet
Always specify the magenta chroma-key color explicitly in prompts rather than requesting generic transparent backgrounds from the image model
Keep animation transform deltas small at 4 pixels or 4 degrees maximum to avoid distracting motion that hurts the pet experience
anti_patterns: Do not request generic transparent backgrounds from GPT Image 2, which produces near-white fills instead of true alpha transparency
Do not let the model freestyle proportions, which produces tall narrow characters that do not fit the required 192 by 208 cell dimensions
Do not mix multiple visual styles in a single prompt, which causes inconsistent and unusable output

faq.question: What is a Codex Pet?
faq.answer: A Codex Pet is a pixel-art animated companion that floats over your desktop while Codex codes. Custom pets are folders containing pet.json and spritesheet.webp placed under the .codex/pets directory.

faq.question: Do I need Codex Pro to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill uses RunComfy CLI with a single RUNCOMFY_TOKEN instead of Codex Pro or the internal imagegen system skill.

faq.question: How long does pet generation take?
faq.answer: Approximately two minutes total. One GPT Image 2 edit call takes about ninety seconds, plus a few seconds for ImageMagick atlas assembly.

faq.question: Why does the skill use only one API call for 72 frames?
faq.answer: Codex Pet animation is intentionally subtle. One canonical pose plus deterministic ImageMagick micro-transforms produces the same visual feel without burning seventy-two separate generation calls.

faq.question: Can I create a pet from a non-human subject?
faq.answer: Yes. Pets, mascots, objects, and food items all work. The base prompt automatically simplifies any subject into the Codex Pet house style.

faq.question: What if my pet loses important identity details?
faq.answer: Re-run the canonical pose generation with a tighter identity-preservation prompt that names specific features such as hair color, glasses, or accessories. The remaining steps are deterministic and do not need changes.
